subject: [CB Budgeting] CB Jan'26 Cycle Submissions - Preliminary

content.answer:
Hi Ian and Jianghong,


Pls find the intended submission of CB this cycle budget submission for
FY26 cut off as of Jan 26th, also attached materials for tmr's walkthrough


#1 [FY26] CB Topline: CB ADO: FY26 at 3mil, +1% vs last submission

   - [BR] +7% vs last submission, to reflect latest CB penetration, FY26 at
   5.8% (last cycle CB penetration proj. 5.3% was benchmark Dec’25, when CB
   penetration normally low during holiday seasons)
   - [TH] -3% vs last submission, to refresh TH import tax impact based on
   actual performance, with CNCB 3P SLS seeing ~22% order drop vs Jan H1 BAU
   days (last cycle factored in ~16% drop)

#2 CB P&L:

   -

   MP Revenue: FY26 MP Rev TR% at 20.8%, +0.2p.p vs last submission to
   factor in
   -

      [ALL]: KRJP Commission Fee increase from Feb’26 and Compulsory Ads
      from Mar’26
      -

      [VN]: Non-ads takerate +0.7p.p mainly from 3PF higher Platform
      Support Fee/GMV % when ABS drop
      -

   SLS: FY26 P&L at $296mil, vs. last submission +10mil. Gaps mainly driven
   by topline changes:
   -

      [PH]: +2.4m from higher 3P SLS orders (+2.0m) from LFF downward
      revision (-2% ADO) and UE improvement (+1.3m) mainly from lower LH and CC
      vendor price down
      -

      [MY]: +3.0m mainly from higher 3P SLS orders (+4.2%) from 3PF
      downward revision (ADO -6%) offset by LH cost increase
(+0.06/kg, -$0.9mil)
      -

      [TH]: +3.2m mainly from UE improvements (+2.1m) from lower LH+CC
      quotation vs last cycle and FX gain (+1.5m), net off by Jan tax
loss due to
      import duty under collection (-3m). *Note that despite we charge
      import duty tax with a $0.38/pc tax surplus, this can not be
booked in SLS
      P&L per Sea Fin/tax guidance
      -

      [SG]: +0.7m mainly from lower WH X-sell orders (-27% vs last cycle,
      PPO is negative) (+2.1m), offset by CNCB 3P PPO drop from higher
      BSC%(+3.4%) from launch of SPX

Hi Ian,

Pls find* KRCB **Impact from TH import duty* tax as attached, this is the
follow-up of CB budget meeting on sharing separately of KR impact of TH tax.


*Faye Yan*

Shopee | Cross Border

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 2

   - For KR, is there any chance we can get discounted rate similar to
   CNCB?
How do we go about doing so?

content.answer:
for local sip, think the same, we move more sales to th fbs, impact to
local sip should also big.

How's the study going? when can we review?

Thanks

for local sip, think the same, we move more sales to th fbs, impact to
local sip should also big.

How's the study going? when can we review?

Thanks

Hi Ian


Pls find reply as follows


From Ian: For KR, is there any chance we can get a discounted rate similar
to CNCB? How do we go about doing so? We probably need to move more TH
sellers to LFF, the impact is quite big.

think your suggestion to move to LFF is more recommended rather than giving
a discounted tax charge, reasons being

   -

   KR is air channel which CC vendors needs to pay to customs that align
   with official announced tax rate, so if to keep the discounted tax rate,
   the tax under collection loss needs to be funded by Shopee (which is the
   Jan 19-Feb 8th case)
   -

   However, the investment is *not so rewarding*, we need to spend *135k
   usd *monthly more vs we charge full tax, but very small ADO/ADGMV uplift
   (see table 1 yellow highlighted)


Table 1: KR 3P SLS ADO/P&L simulation by different tax cost (normalized,
topline performances using actual change results)
[image: image.png]
Best,

*Faye Yan*

Shopee | Cross Border

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

content.question: I see, we cannot do it how CNCB does it?

*From:* Faye Yan <faye.yan@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Friday, 30 January 2026 7:17 pm
*To:* Soo Siang, Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Haenle Suh <haenle.suh@shopee.com>; Amanda Eng <amanda.eng@shopee.com>;
Ray Wang <ray.wang01@shopee.com>; Junho Kim <junho.kim@shopee.com>; Shuning
Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Yao Jiaqi <jiaqi.yao@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: [CB Budgeting] CB Jan'26 Cycle Submissions - Preliminary



Hi Ian



Pls find reply as follows



From Ian: For KR, is there any chance we can get a discounted rate similar
to CNCB?
How do we go about doing so?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Remove rest to answer your question.

I see, we cannot do it how CNCB does it?

with TH customs, so,


   - from P&L wise, we will have* tax over collection if we charge 100% tax
   revenue but pay 70% tax cost to customs,* and by right, we shouldn't
   "overcharge" tax and spend the tax gain
   - from counter competitor wise, which is more importantly, TT is
   charging sellers by discounted rate, to avoid seller confusion + lose
   NPC/SOB, need to go with discount rate

Best,

*Faye Yan*

Shopee | Cross Border

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265
